exampleSentences: Anh thích lẫn cà phê lẫn trà vào buổi sáng.
Chị ấy nói được lẫn tiếng Anh lẫn tiếng Pháp rất tốt.
Trong bữa tiệc, em gặp lẫn bạn cũ lẫn bạn mới.
notes:
lẫn most often appears in the pattern “lẫn A lẫn B” = “both A and B.” It links two things and stresses that both are included.

You can’t use lẫn alone to mean “both” — it normally comes in this pair structure. It’s similar to cả… lẫn… and sometimes close to vừa… vừa… (but vừa… vừa… often implies two actions or qualities at the same time).

As a verb, lẫn can also mean “to mix” or “to confuse (with)” — like mixing things together or mixing them up mentally.

content.sentence: Tôi không muốn ___ giữa hai người.

translation.en: I don't want to mix between two people.
I don't want to mix between two people.

content.sentence: Tôi không muốn ___ giữa hai người.

notes_md:
**lẫn** most often appears in the pattern **“lẫn A lẫn B” = “both A and B.”** It links two things and stresses that *both are included*.


You can’t use **lẫn** alone to mean “both” — it normally comes in this pair structure. It’s similar to **cả… lẫn…** and sometimes close to **vừa… vừa…** (but *vừa… vừa…* often implies two actions or qualities at the same time).

As a verb, **lẫn** can also mean “to mix” or “to confuse (with)” — like mixing things together or mixing them up mentally.

example.en: I mix up the two colors.
example.vi: Tôi lẫn lộn giữa hai màu sắc.

definitions.vi: Làm lẫn lộn hoặc nhầm lẫn.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He likes both coffee and tea in the morning.
She can speak both English and French very well.
At the party, I met both old friends and new friends.
